title: The agent loop
summary: An agent alternates between deciding, using tools, observing results, and deciding again.
body_html: An agent is an LLM system allowed to choose steps, call tools, read observations, and continue until a task is done or stopped. The loop is powerful because it can adapt mid-task, but every loop also creates chances for stale context, wrong tool calls, and unchecked assumptions.
key_points: Agents need tools, state, stopping rules, and review points.
Observations should update the plan, not merely decorate it.
More autonomy trades predictability for reach.

steps.thought: Find the latest API shape before editing.
Patch the call site from memory because the docs look familiar.
steps.observation: Changed amount and id, but did not include refundReason.

exercise.explain: The agent observed a required field, then edited from memory and failed to carry that fact into the patch.

outcome: You can read a simple agent trace and spot where the loop stopped using evidence.
